items.t: — Friday's late save at $3,959 held; the floor at $4,000 now goes to trial in Fed week, December hike odds near 82%
— June gems and jewellery exports hit $2.21B; gold jewellery +54.5%, polished diamonds +8.7% in the category's first clean rise
— Botswana says its 15% of De Beers carries 'complete freedom': join the Penny consortium, pre-empt alone, or bring a third party
— TÜV Austria certifies all 29.8M carats of 2025 output carbon-neutral, a second straight year; G7 sanctions still apply
— Gemfields' first Trade Select auction clears 348,409 carats at 93.2% sold for $23.1M; sapphire categories debut
— the white metal leads Monday's tape, +1.67% before New York opens; palladium +1.46% to $1,248
— Berkshire's jewelry arm gives revenue to Arien Gessner and the supply chain to Moss Makhoulian; one owner per side of the squeeze
— Studer counts the footprint in travel and freight, not steel; new packaging cut emissions 50%
— only 30% of UK retailers haven't tried AI, per ONS data; 53.6% of adopters run it on free tools
— Swiss watch export figures for June are due from the FH on Tuesday; the trade watches whether America's run offsets Asia
